Name: Absol G
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Return all Energy cards attached to Absol G to your hand. The Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out at the end of your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Blaziken FB
Effects: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
If your opponent has any [W] Pokémon in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Drifblim FB
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent has 3 or less Prize cards left, Drifblim FB gets +40 HP.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

Name: Electivire FB
Effects: Discard up to 2 Energy cards from your hand. For each card you discarded, draw 2 cards.
Move a [L] Energy card attached to Electivire FB to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Garchomp
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Garchomp is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent’s attack (even if Garchomp is Knocked Out), choose an Energy card attached to the Attacking Pokémon and put it into your opponent’s hand.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Garchomp by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Does 120 damage minus 20 damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Magmortar
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Magmortar from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may move a [R] Energy card attached to Magmortar to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Metagross
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) gets -20 HP. No more than 20 HP can be reduced by all Gravitation Poké-Bodies.
Effects: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that is the same type as the Defending Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

Name: Rayquaza C
Effects: You may discard up to 5 Energy cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy card you discarded.
Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Regigigas FB
Effects: Remove from Regigigas FB a number of damage counters equal to the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Does 20 damage times the number of different types of Pokémon SP you have in play.

Name: Rhyperior
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Rhyperior.
If the Defending Pokémon would be Knocked Out by this attack, discard the top 3 cards from your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It can launch a rock held in its hand like a missile by tightening then expanding muscles instantly.

Name: Staraptor FB
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

Name: Swampert
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Swampert by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Before doing damage, you may switch your opponent’s Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Does 60 damage plus 10 more damage for each [F] Energy attached to Swampert.

Name: Venusaur
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your [G] Pokémon. Each of your [G] Pokémon can’t be affected by any Special Conditions.
Effects: If Venusaur already has 8 or more damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.
If the Defending Pokémon is affected by any Special Conditions, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage for each of those Special Conditions.

Name: Yanmega
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Yanmega is your Active Pokémon, you may search your discard pile for a [G] Energy card and attach it to Yanmega. This power can’t be used if Yanmega is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may return all [G] Energy attached to Yanmega to your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy card you returned.

Name: Arcanine G
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Arcanine G‘s Retreat Cost is [C] less for each [R] Energy attached to Arcanine G.
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A legendary bird Pokémon. It can create blizzards by freezing moisture in the air.

Name: Butterfree FB
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, each of Butterfree FB‘s attacks does 30 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Choose either Burned or Poisoned. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: You may move a [R] Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon to Camerupt.
Flip 3 coins. For each tails, discard the top card of your deck. Ignore this effect if your opponent has any [W] Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: It has volcanoes on its back. If magma builds up in its body, it shudders, then erupts violently.

Name: Camerupt G
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Charizard G
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Remove 3 damage counters from each of your Pokémon.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. Count the amount of Energy attached to that Pokémon. Put that many damage counters on the Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cries echo inside its hollow body to emerge as beautiful notes for startling and repelling foes.

Name: Claydol
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on any Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) in any way you like.
If the Defending Pokémon has the same remaining HP as Claydol, this attack’s base damage is 90 instead of 30.
Flavor-Text: An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

Name: Crawdaunt G
Effects: You may switch Crawdaunt G with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Dewgong
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done to Dewgong by attacks from [R] Pokémon and [W] Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a [F] Pokémon, this attack’s base damage is 80 instead of 30.

Name: Dodrio
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card. This power can’t be used if Dodrio is affected by a Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: When Doduo evolves into this odd breed, one of its heads splits into two. It runs at nearly 40 mph.

Name: Dusknoir FB
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Does 10 damage times the number of Pokémon SP in your discard pile.

Name: Empoleon FB
Effects: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon.
If you played any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Exploud
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Each of your Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Honchkrow
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Honchkrow is your Active Pokémon and your opponent’s Bench isn’t full, you may use this power. Search your opponent’s discard pile for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto his or her bench. This power can’t be used if Honchkrow is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon that isn’t an evolved Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called “Summoner of Night.”

Name: Lickilicky C
Effects: Attach a Basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, remove 2 damage counters from that Pokémon.
Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

Name: Lucario C
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

Name: Lunatone
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have Solrock in play, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to any of your Lunatone or Solrock by your opponent’s Pokémon LV.X.
Effects: Does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that doesn’t have a Retreat Cost. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.

Name: Mawile
Effects: During your next turn, any damage done by Mawile’s Swallow or Bite to your opponent’s Active Pokémon is increased by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Remove from Mawile the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Your opponent draws 2 cards.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It gains the ability to see the aura of its opponents by honing its mind through starvation.

Name: Milotic C
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to Milotic C. This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Moltres
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. It is said that its appearance indicates the coming of spring.

Name: Mr. Mime
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Mr. Mime would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack that does 70 or more damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), Mr. Mime is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Effects: If Mr. Mime already has 5 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

Name: Parasect
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [G] Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A mushroom grown larger than the host’s body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent’s deck. If you discarded a Pokémon, this attack does damage equal to the HP of that Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

Name: Roserade C
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to Roserade C, remove all Special Conditions from Roserade C.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and remove 2 damage counters from Roserade C.

Name: Sableye G
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

Name: Sandslash
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose as many [F] Energy cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Sandslash is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It curls up, then rolls into foes with its back. Its sharp spines inflict severe damage.

Name: Seaking
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, if Seaking would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Seaking is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Flavor-Text: In autumn, its body becomes more fatty in preparing to propose to a mate. It takes on beautiful colors.

Name: Shedinja
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Shedinja by your opponent’s Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one’s spirit.

Name: Solrock
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Lunatone in play, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order. This power can’t be used if Solrock is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: If Lunatone is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. Then, search your discard pile for Lunatone, show it to your opponent, and shuffle it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: A new Pokémon species, rumored to be from the sun. It gives off light while spinning.

Name: Spinda
Effects: If any Energy card attached to Spinda is the same type as any Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon, discard 1 of those Energy cards from the Defending Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack’s base damage is 10 instead of 30.
Flavor-Text: No two Spinda have the same pattern of spots. Its tottering step fouls the aim of foes.

Name: Wailord
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Wailord. Wailord is now Asleep.
Wailord can’t use Giant Wave during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

Name: Altaria C
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.

Name: Arcanine
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

Name: Bibarel
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.

Name: Breloom
Effects: The Retreat Cost for the Defending Pokémon is 0 until the end of your next turn.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage. Then, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, remove from Carnivine the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

Name: Chatot G
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you put Chatot G from your hand onto your Bench, you may look at the top 4 cards of your opponent’s deck. Put them back on top of your opponent’s deck in any order.
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put Chatot G and all cards attached to it on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Cherrim
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the numbers of heads. Cherrim is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun’s rays with its whole body.

Name: Dragonite FB
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon SP, this attack’s base damage is 80 instead of 20.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Drifblim
Flavor-Text: At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is a common sight around fishing ports. It is known to rescue people and carry off prey.

Name: Gabite
Effects: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It loves sparkly things. It seeks treasures in caves and hoards the loot in its nest.

Name: Garchomp C
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

Name: Ivysaur
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Ivysaur from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may use this power. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: When the bud on its back starts swelling, a sweet aroma wafts to indicate the flower’s coming bloom.

Name: Lopunny
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

Name: Loudred
Effects: Discard the top card from your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

Name: Magmar
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Magmar.
Flavor-Text: When it breathes deeply, heat waves form around its body, making it hard to see clearly.

Name: Manectric G
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to Manectric G.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Marshtomp
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Marshtomp is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, move all Energy cards attached to your Active Pokémon to Marshtomp. If you do, switch Marshtomp with that Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

Name: Masquerain
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: As long as Masquerain is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.

Name: Metang
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Metang has any [M] Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Metang is 0.
Effects: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is formed by two Beldum joining together. Its steel body won’t be scratched if it collides with a jet.

Name: Milotic
Effects: You may discard 2 cards from your hand. If you do, remove 4 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

Name: Minun
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Search your discard pile for a [L] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon to another of your opponent’s Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)
Flavor-Text: If spotted, it will lure an unwary person into chasing it, then lose the pursuer on mountain trails.

Name: Ninjask
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Switch Ninjask with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Because it moves so quickly, it sometimes becomes unseeable. It congregates around tree sap.

Name: Numel
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 2 [R] Energy cards and attach them to Numel.
Flavor-Text: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

Name: Pinsir
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent returns the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it to his or her hand.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can’t tear, it tosses far.

Name: Plusle
Effects: If you have the same number of cards or less in your hand as your opponent, draw cards until you have 1 more card than your opponent. (If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does nothing.)
Attach a [L] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.
Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Raticate G
Effects: Search your discard pile for a Trainer card or a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
Flip a coin, if heads this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon for each Pokémon Tool and Stadium card your opponent has in play. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Standing on its hind legs freed its forelegs and made it smarter. It is very forgetful, however.

Name: Roserade
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Each of its hands contains different toxins, but both hands can jab with near-fatal power.

Name: Rotom
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Rotom. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

Name: Skarmory FB
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, when your opponent puts a Basic Pokémon from his or her hand onto his or her Bench, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Discard all [M] Energy attached to Skarmory FB. Flip a coin for each Energy card you discarded. This attack does 20 damage plus 40 damage for each heads.

Name: Spiritomb C
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Recognizing their own weakness, they always live in a group. When alone, a Staravia cries noisily.

Name: Togekiss C
Effects: Draw a card.
Togekiss C does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Wailmer
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Wailmer but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 20 damage in this way.
Wailmer does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to Yanma.
Flavor-Text: It can hover is one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

Name: Beldum
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It converses with others by using magnetic pulses. In a swarm, they move in perfect unison.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: A comparison revealed that Bidoof’s front teeth grow at the same rate as Rattata’s.

Name: Buizel
Flavor-Text: It spins its two tails like a screw to propel itself through water. The tails also slice clinging seaweed.

Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: For some time after its birth, it grows by gaining nourishment from the seed on its back.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Switch Buneary with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Remove 1 damage counter from Buneary.
Flavor-Text: Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Chatot does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its tongue is just like a human’s. As a result, it can cleverly mimic human speech.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: Attach a [G] Energy card from your hand to Cherubi.
Flavor-Text: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is very agile. Before going to sleep, it extinguishes the flame on its tail to prevent fires.

Name: Chingling
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chimecho from your hand onto Chingling (this counts as evolving Chingling) and remove all damage counters from Chingling.
Effects: Search your opponent’s discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your opponent’s discard pile.)
Flavor-Text: There is an orb inside its mouth. When it hops, the orb bounces all over and makes a ringing sound.

Name: Combee
Effects: Remove 2 damage counters from Combee.
Flavor-Text: The trio is together from birth. It constantly gathers honey from flowers to please Vespiquen.

Name: Corphish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

Name: Croagunk
Flavor-Text: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

Name: Doduo
Effects: Doduo does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Drifloon does 10 damage to itself.
Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.”

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Pokémon that isn’t an Evolved Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

Name: Geodude
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.

Name: Gible
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It attacks using its huge mouth. While its attacks are powerful, it hurts itself out of clumsiness, too.

Name: Goldeen
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Goldeen.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Though it appears very elegant when swimming with fins unfurled, it can jab powerfully with its horn.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Growlithe.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon with a loyal nature. It will remain motionless until it is given an order by its Trainer.

Name: Kricketot
Flavor-Text: Its legs are short. Whenever it stumbles, its stiff antennae clack with a xylophone-like sound.

Name: Magikarp
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A Magikarp living for many years can leap a mountain using Splash. The move remains useless, though.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The faster the units at its sides rotate, the greater the magnetic force they generate.

Name: Mankey
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon LV.X, this attack does 10 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Does 30 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Meditite.
Flavor-Text: It always trains deep in mountains. It levitates when it heightens its spiritual power through meditation.

Name: Meowth
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

Name: Mime Jr.
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Mr. Mime from your hand onto Mime Jr. (this counts as evolving Mime Jr.) and remove all damage counters from Mime Jr.
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can use only that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It mimics the expressions and motions of those it sees to understand the feelings of others.

Name: Mudkip
Effects: If Mudkip has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

Name: Nincada
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It grows underground, sensing its surroundings using antennae instead of its virtually blind eyes.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Paras
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Mushrooms named tochukaso grow on its back. They grow along with the host Paras.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

Name: Piplup
Flavor-Text: A poor walker, it often falls down. However, its strong pride makes it puff up its chest without a care.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its powerful tackles can destroy anything. However, it is too slow witted to help people work.

Name: Roselia
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The more healthy the Roselia, the more pleasant its flowers’ aroma. Its scent deeply relaxes people.

Name: Sandshrew
Flavor-Text: To protect itself from attackers, it curls up into a ball. It lives in arid regions with minimal rainfall.

Name: Seel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives on icebergs. It swims in the sea using the point on its head to break up ice.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Shroomish
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: As soon as the tail claws close, its needle tips secrete poison. It can survive a year without food.

Name: Starly
Flavor-Text: Because they are weak individually, they form groups. However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

Name: Surskit
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It appears as if it is skating on water. It draws prey with a sweet scent from the tip of its head.

Name: Turtwig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The shell on its back is made of soil. On a very healthy Turtwig, the shell should feel moist.

Name: Whismur
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs. If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It checks its surroundings and location using reflections of the ultrasonic waves from its mouth.

Name: Battle Tower
Description: Whenever any player plays any Pokémon from his or her hand to Level-Up 1 of his or her Pokémon, remove 4 damage counters from that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Champion’s Room
Description: The Retreat cost for each Pokémon SP (both yours and your opponent’s) is [C] less.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Cynthia’s Guidance
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Cyrus’s Initiative
Description: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, look at your opponent’s hand. For each heads, choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand and put it on the bottom of your opponent’s deck in any order.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Night Teleporter
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put all cards in your hand on top of your deck. Then, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Palmer’s Contribution
Description: Search your discard pile for up to 5 in any combination of Pokémon and basic Energy cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: VS Seeker
Description: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

Name: Absol G LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Absol G LV.X from your hand onto your Active Absol G, you may flip 3 coins. For each heads, put the top card from your opponent’s deck in the Lost Zone.
Effects: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Absol G. Absol G LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Blaziken FB LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: Any damage done by attacks to a Burned Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). No more than 40 damage can be added by all Burning Spirit Poké-Bodies.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to Blaziken FB by attacks is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Blaziken FB. Blaziken FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Charizard G LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move an Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to Charizard G. This power can’t be used if Charizard G is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Energy attached to Charizard G.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Charizard G. Charizard G LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Electivire FB LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. If you do, your turn ends. Search your discard pile for up to 3 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. This power can’t be used if Electivire FB is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Electivire FB. Electivire FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Garchomp C LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you put Garchomp C LV.X from your hand onto your Active Garchomp C, you may remove all damage counters from each of your Pokémon SP.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy attached to Garchomp C. Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Garchomp C can’t use Dragon Rush during your next turn.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Garchomp C. Garchomp C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Rayquaza C LV.X
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If Rayquaza C is your Active Pokémon and is damaged but not Knocked Out by an opponent’s attack, you may search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Rayquaza C.
Effects: Discard all Energy attached to Rayquaza C. Ignore this effect if you have no cards in your hand.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Rayquaza C. Rayquaza C LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Staraptor FB LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can’t be used if Staraptor FB is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Before doing damage, you may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, this attack’s base damage is 70 instead of 40.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Staraptor FB. Staraptor FB LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to freeze the water in the air during winter, causing snow.

Name: Moltres
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a [R] Energy attached to Moltres. If tails, discard all Energy attached to Moltres.
Flavor-Text: The flames on this legendary Pokémon’s wings burn so brightly that they can make night seem like day.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If tails, Zapdos does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon is said to be present wherever there is a lightning storm.

Name: Milotic
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have no cards in your hand, prevent all damage done to Milotic by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Milotic.
Flavor-Text: Its lovely scales are described as rainbow colored. They change color depending on the viewing angle.

Name: Relicanth
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] more.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

Name: Yanma
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance.
You may switch Yanma with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, move as many Energy cards attached to Yanma as you like to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can hover in one spot by flapping its wings at high speed. It flits about to guard its territory.
